title: ControlChange
content:
# ControlChange

Inherits from: ApiServiceModel


## Methods


### get_control_change_clustering(data: Optional[Union[requests.ControlChangeClusteringGet, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> KList[responses.ControlChangeClustering]

Retrieve the total count of Control Changes matching an array of `resources` and filter options between two dates grouped by the parameter `time_bucket`. Will also return a list of all the Control Change `id`s counted.


### create_control_change(data: Optional[Union[requests.ControlChangeCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.ControlChangeCreate

Send a new value to be written to an Asset / Data Stream pair. The Control Change Manager will automatically find which Cluster and Bridge to communicate the change to.


### get_control_change_last(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.ControlChangeLastGet, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[responses.ControlChangeGet], responses.ControlChangeLastGetPaginatedResponseCursor]

Returns a dictionary with a data property containing an array of latest Control Change objects. Only the latest Control Changes for each `resource` in the request filters will be returned.


### list_control_changes(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.ControlChangesList, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[responses.ControlChangeGet], responses.ControlChangesListPaginatedResponseCursor]

Returns a list of Control Change objects for specific Asset / Data Stream pairs or Control Change IDs. The list can be optionally filtered and sorted on the server before being returned.


### get_control_change_range(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.ControlChangeRangeGet, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[responses.ControlChangeGet], responses.ControlChangeRangeGetPaginatedResponseCursor]

Returns a dictionary with a data property containing an array of Control Changes within a specified time range for all of the `resources` in the `resources` array that match the filter options.


### delete_control_change(control_change_id: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Deletes a control change


### get_control_change(control_change_id: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.ControlChangeGet

Retrieves the properties, status and all associated logs of a Control Change.

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client()
result = client.client.api.control_change.get_control_change_clustering()
